title: Caldera - Exfiltration of Staged Data over C2 Channel (Linux)
description: Detects Linux-side exfiltration of collected data over the C2 channel using HTTP POST requests. Caldera T1041 emulation transmits staged files from /tmp or /dev/shm back to the C2 server using curl or wget with POST/PUT methods and multipart form uploads, distinct from the alternative-protocol T1048 pattern.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (( ((msg like '*curl -X POST*' or msg like '*curl --upload-file*' or msg like '*curl -F*' or msg like '*curl -d @*' or msg like '*curl -T *')) or ((msg like '*wget --post-file*' or msg like '*wget --post-data*')) or ((msg like '*requests.post*' or msg like '*urllib.request.urlopen*' or msg like '*http.client.HTTPConnection*')) ) and ((msg like '*/tmp/*' or msg like '*/dev/shm/*' or msg like '*/var/tmp/*')))
falsepositives: Application deployment pipelines uploading build artifacts
Monitoring agents posting metrics to collection endpoints
